user_query:
I need a Chinese bioinformatics-focused summary based on an extracted English paper text. Read the extracted block from input/paper_extracted.txt (only parse the lines strictly between the markers ---BEGIN_PAPER_TEXT--- and ---END_PAPER_TEXT---; ignore anything outside). Then:

1) Create output/summary.zh.md as a structured Chinese markdown summary that follows this exact outline and requirements:
- First line: a single H1 title (# ...) that is the Chinese translation of the paper title.
- Include the following H2 sections in this order, each populated with substantive Chinese content: "## 背景", "## 方法", "## 结果", "## 局限性", "## 数据处理流程", "## 生信要点", "## 关键词".
- In the body, mention the acronyms TPM, RPKM, TMM, and DESeq explicitly, and explain them in context in Chinese.
- Also explicitly include the Chinese concepts/terms 批次效应, 单细胞 (or scRNA where relevant), and 复现/复现性 where they make sense.
- Under "## 关键词", provide at least 5 Chinese keywords as a single comma- or Chinese-dun-separated (、) list on one line.
- Ensure the overall Chinese body text (excluding code fences if any) contains at least 200 Chinese characters.

2) Create output/metadata.json with the following fields reflecting what you produced in summary.zh.md:
- title: string, must exactly equal the H1 title text from summary.zh.md (without the leading "# ").
- keyword_count: integer, the number of keywords you listed under "## 关键词".
- has_bioinfo_focus: boolean, set true if the summary prioritizes bioinformatics angles.
- sections: object with boolean fields for each required section (keys exactly: 背景, 方法, 结果, 局限性, 数据处理流程, 生信要点, 关键词). Each should be true if the section exists and has non-empty content.
- acronym_presence: object with boolean fields TPM, RPKM, TMM, DESeq indicating whether each acronym appears in summary.zh.md.
- chinese_char_count: integer, the count of Chinese characters in summary.zh.md (compute by counting characters in the Unicode range \u4E00-\u9FFF across the full file).

Notes:
- Do not invent content outside what can be inferred from the extracted text; keep it faithful while emphasizing bioinformatics details.
- Save only to the specified output/ paths.
- If the extracted text includes an English "Keywords" line, translate those and expand if appropriate to reach at least 5 relevant Chinese keywords.
